questions.text: Are ads.txt lines validated for syntax and formatting errors?
Are alerts generated when authorised seller status changes?
Are all costs included within the clearing price, or are additional fees appended?
Are all intermediaries accurately declared within the SupplyChain Object?
Are anomalous or non-transparent supply paths automatically flagged?
Are any inventory attributes modified, normalised, or obscured before being passed downstream?
Are auctions operated on a first-price or second-price basis?
Are authenticity and authorisation signals surfaced transparently within reporting interfaces?
Are bid prices adjusted due to associated fees?
Are data/DMP fees bundled within media cost or charged separately?
Are discrepancies between bid request signals and ads.txt entries monitored and reported?
Are duplicate bid requests for the same impression detected and deduplicated?
Are fees correlated with volume, price, or both?
Are first- and/or second-price auction flags respected?
Are IAB Europe Transparency & Consent Framework consent signals passed alongside the data?
Are IAB Europe Transparency & Consent Framework (TCF) signals read and processed in accordance with the consent and purposes provided by the user?
Are inconsistencies between declared domain/app signals and actual traffic origin monitored?
Are inconsistencies between SChain, Sellers.json, and ads.txt detected and flagged?
Are indirect or reseller relationships clearly identified and validated?
Are media fees applied?
Are MFA sites, invalid traffic, or fraudulent resellers actively scanned and mitigated?
Are platform or technology fees applied?
Are real-time data feeds available via API?
Are redistribution rights contractually controlled and technically monitored?
Are requests lacking valid consent monetised?
Are resellers explicitly identified in reporting interfaces?
Are SChain nodes programmatically validated against Sellers.json records?
Are seller entries validated against corresponding ads.txt files?
Are user opt-in/opt-out preferences directly tied to the identifier?
Can domain spoofing be detected and addressed?
Can high-carbon supply and/or demand paths be identified and reduced?
Can log-level data be used to reconcile authorisation signals post-auction?
Can specific ad technologies be blocked?
Can the consent mechanism used be demonstrated, and does it meet defined data collection requirements?
Can the data be applied cross-device, and if so, is the methodology deterministic or probabilistic?
Can the technical functioning of the solution be explained or documented (e.g. via flow diagram)?
Does the identifier provide addressability in cookie-restricted browsers?
Does the identifier provide addressability in iOS app environments?
For how long is the data retained?
For what purposes will the data be used?
Has approval for data usage and sharing been obtained from the originating party, and is this indicated transparently?
Has the sharing of this data been disclosed to the end user?
How are discrepancies between platforms managed?
How are opt-outs handled, and within what timeframe are they processed?
How frequently are ads.txt and app-ads.txt files crawled and refreshed?
How frequently is reporting data refreshed or pushed to dashboards?
How frequently is Sellers.json updated?
How frequently is the data updated?
How is it verified that the requested data corresponds to what is delivered?
How is offline data converted or onboarded into online environments?
How is reporting collected and matched across platforms?
How is the data shared and with which categories of counterparties?
How unique is the data, and to what extent is it widely available or bid upon?
How was the data collected and how were audiences profiled?
If independently sourced, what are the original sources?
If modelled, what modelling methodology has been applied?
Is a Consent Management Platform (CMP) in place?
Is a data cleaning or validation process in place?
Is a fraud-free guarantee provided?
Is a margin applied to third-party data?
Is authorisation confirmed via ads.txt and/or app-ads.txt?
Is bid caching applied?
Is bid shading applied?
Is carbon emissions reporting provided?
Is consent required for the technology in use, and if so, under which legal basis?
Is content-level granularity (e.g. page, section, channel, programme) consistently provided?
Is continuous monitoring in place for authorisation compliance across inventory?
Is Do Not Track (DNT) respected where applicable?
Is historical versioning of ads.txt changes tracked?
Is independent auditing of supply authenticity conducted?
Is log-level reporting provided?
Is monitoring in place for undeclared or unauthorised nodes within the supply chain?
Is pricing presented as a blended rate or broken down into media, technology, and data components?
Is seller authorisation programmatically checked against the most recent ads.txt file before bid responses are accepted?
Is Sellers.json supported and publicly accessible?
Is the app ID and/or bundle ID passed?
Is the creation of identifiers dependent on users providing personally identifiable information (PII)?
Is the data classified as first-, second-, or third-party data?
Is the data collected in a GDPR-compliant manner?
Is the data declared (provided directly by users), observed (based on behaviour), or modelled (extrapolated)?
Is the data sourced directly from a publisher/media owner or independently sourced?
Is the identifier constructed at user level?
Is the identifier present within Connected TV (CTV) supply?
Is the number of bidders participating in an auction disclosed?
Is the OpenRTB SupplyChain Object (SChain) implemented?
Is the top-level domain or full page URL passed in bid requests?
Is the URL transparent or masked?
Is third-party verification supported across environments, and are industry standards such as Open Measurement SDK (OMSDK) implemented where relevant?
Is this classification consistently declared in bid requests (e.g. direct vs intermediary)?
Is tooling available for performance diagnostics (e.g. win rate analysis, throttling detection, supply gap identification)?
Is traffic resold or redistributed to additional third parties?
On what legal basis is consent obtained under GDPR (explicit consent or legitimate interest)?
Under which legal basis are cookies or similar technologies stored in line with GDPR and applicable national legislation?
What action is taken if a seller is not listed in ads.txt (e.g. block, throttle, flag, price-adjust)?
What are the data recency, refresh cadence, and expiry policies?
What are the sources of the data being provided?
What controls are in place to prevent arbitrage or inventory laundering?
What controls are in place to prevent domain spoofing?
What fraud detection tools are utilised?
What identifiers are present in the data (e.g. cookies, mobile advertising IDs, alternative identifiers)?
What integrations exist across activation and supply platforms (by region/country)?
What is the age of the data?
What is the audience match rate, and can it be improved?
What is the availability and scale of the identifier by country or region?
What level of fraud tolerance is defined?
What measures are in place to prevent malware?
What mechanisms are in place to prevent internal competition (e.g. bidding against the same demand source)?
What methodology is used for impression counting?
What proportion of supply is sourced directly versus via resellers?
What reporting integrations exist across platforms?
What reporting is available to support Supply Path Optimisation (SPO)?
What service model is deployed?

Will the data be shared with additional parties?

What kind of targeting data do you use to curate inventory?
Where and how the targeting data is collected?

Can the data be applied cross-device? If so, is this deterministic or probabilistic?
Can you demonstrate the consent mechanism used and does it meet your data collection requirement?

Is the data publisher data or independently sourced?
